name: Husqvarna Automower
short_description: A line of autonomous robotic lawn mowers that navigate and cut a yard on their own, returning to a charging station as needed.
long_description: First sold in 1995 as the solar-powered Automower and refined across decades, Husqvarna's Automower is one of the longest-running consumer autonomous robots, quietly mowing lawns without supervision. Early models stayed inside a buried boundary wire, while recent EPOS and AI-vision models use RTK satellite positioning and an onboard camera to follow virtual boundaries and avoid obstacles with no wires at all. As a familiar appliance that acts on its own in shared outdoor space, it is a mainstream example of physical AI that people delegate real tasks to in everyday domestic life.

images.caption: A Husqvarna Automower 430X robotic lawn mower on a lawn.
images.alt_text: A low, dark-grey wheeled Husqvarna Automower robotic lawn mower sitting on a green lawn.
